description:
Teach the compiler to publish interface facts from the new substrate. `DefineModule` should derive and publish `ModuleInterface(module)` from already-minted callable identities for local source-defined modules, including protocol modules and synthesized helpers like `__info__/1`. Add the runtime/external seeding path so a module can publish `ModuleInterface` without requiring `ModuleDefined`.

Protocol modules should become ordinary module-interface producers: their callback functions belong to the protocol module's callable surface, which downstream protocol facts can then consume by `FunctionId`.

Signal: local, protocol, runtime, and external modules share one interface fact shape, and external-interface consumers no longer need placeholder `ModuleDefined` waits just to see callables.
title: Compiler2 interface publication: publish one callable surface for local, protocol, and runtime/external modules
